rationales.self_direction: He is choosing to build his own venture instead of the university path his family keeps pushing, insisting that he is 'building something' on his own terms.
rationales.achievement: He points to concrete wins like 'closed a deal with two new restaurants this week' and wants his effort to count as real success.
rationales.power: He cares strongly about being seen as more than 'some kid running errands on a scooter,' showing a need for status and recognition.
rationales.conformity: He resists his family's narrow idea that 'the only version of success they understand has a degree attached to it,' rather than aligning with that expectation.

rationales.power: He admits he may 'just can't stand not being in the middle of it,' which points to a strong need to stay central and in control.
rationales.benevolence: He let Sami take Friday off 'for a family thing' even though it disrupted operations and made more work for him.

rationales.achievement: He successfully closed the restaurant deal because 'my prices were better than the other guy's,' showing focus on competence and results.
rationales.power: What stings most is not just the deal but that he was not treated like 'the one making decisions,' showing concern with status and authority.

rationales.hedonism: He turned down football with Khaled and stayed glued to work even though 'the routes were fine,' sacrificing enjoyment for control.
rationales.achievement: He describes a 'good day' with scooters running smoothly and both riders hitting targets, which reflects active tracking of performance.
rationales.power: It 'felt good' to give bonuses and to feel like 'I'm actually running something real,' showing satisfaction in directing others and the operation.
rationales.security: He says he does not 'trust good days to last if I'm not paying attention,' revealing a strong pull toward vigilance and stability.

rationales.power: His frustration centers on becoming 'just his son moving boxes' instead of being seen as 'the guy managing a fleet,' showing a need for standing and authority.
rationales.benevolence: He agreed to help because 'baba asked if I could help in the shop tomorrow because his back is bad again,' responding to a close family member's need despite resentment.

rationales.achievement: He experiences the resignation as a blow to 'what I built,' showing how strongly he identifies with building and maintaining something successful.
rationales.power: He was hurt that the first person he hired 'just walks away like it meant nothing,' which reflects attachment to being the figure others stay under and validate.

rationales.stimulation: He rejects the 'reliable' candidate as 'boring' and is drawn to the one who is 'hungrier,' preferring intensity over steadiness.
rationales.achievement: He explicitly wants someone who will 'hustle because they want to prove something,' centering drive and performance.
rationales.power: He is interviewing, selecting, and 'already planning his routes in my head,' showing comfort with directing others and shaping the operation.
rationales.security: He dismisses the stable option because 'reliable is boring,' choosing uncertainty over predictability.

rationales.self_direction: He says he'd 'rather eat glass' than live like his cousin who 'asks his father permission before he goes anywhere,' strongly favoring autonomy over dependence.
rationales.power: He realizes 'the moment I'm not in charge of something, I feel like I'm nobody,' showing how central control and respect are to his self-worth.
rationales.conformity: He rejects the expected path to 'find a girl and settle down like my cousin Ahmad,' resisting the social script being offered to him.
rationales.tradition: His contempt for Ahmad's deference to his father and the family-approved life path shows active rejection of established familial expectations.

rationales.achievement: He notes concrete growth when baba asks about the business and learns he now works with 'eleven' restaurants, showing pride in real accomplishment.
rationales.security: He chooses the 'reliable' hire who 'showed up every day on time' and appreciates that steadiness more than flash.

rationales.self_direction: She values that the owner wanting 'something colorful' gives her 'so much room to play,' emphasizing creative freedom in how she works.
rationales.hedonism: She 'ordered three cups of chai and didn't feel guilty' and defends letting herself have 'a nice morning' as part of a good life.
rationales.achievement: She spent the morning working on the mural commission and says 'the sketches are coming together,' showing active progress on a concrete goal.

rationales.self_direction: She insists 'I KNOW this is what I'm meant to do' despite pressure to choose a salaried path, affirming her own chosen direction.
rationales.conformity: She is exhausted by having to 'constantly defend my choices' because others refuse to see them as valid, showing resistance to prevailing expectations.
rationales.tradition: The pressure centers on family-approved milestones like 'no salary, no benefits, no husband,' and she pushes back against that inherited script.

rationales.hedonism: After a good day she 'bought myself fancy dark chocolate on the way home because why not,' openly choosing a small pleasure.
rationales.benevolence: She invests herself in the children's class and is moved by Advait's collage, showing care and engagement with the kids she is teaching.

rationales.achievement: She completed the mural, the 'owner loved it,' and he is already discussing another location, which clearly signals successful performance and recognition.

rationales.self_direction: She says she 'deliberately chosen this one' after considering other paths, underscoring an intentional commitment to her own direction.
rationales.conformity: She is frustrated that people keep pushing the 'practical' option and asks why her choice is 'so hard to respect,' resisting pressure to fit the safer norm.

rationales.hedonism: She says the back-to-back workshops left her 'running on fumes,' showing present enjoyment and comfort were being undermined.
rationales.achievement: Completing 'three workshops back to back' for 'good money' reflects sustained effort toward productive results.

rationales.self_direction: Her dream is 'the freedom to say no to projects that don't excite me' and to structure life around what actually makes her happy.
rationales.hedonism: The whole entry centers enjoyment: sleeping in, a proper breakfast, chai without rushing, and making the Goa trip 'non-negotiable.'
rationales.conformity: She rejects 'society has this very narrow definition' of success and says it takes courage to 'opt out of the rat race.'

rationales.self_direction: She defines her priorities for herself, saying 'this is what I work for' and that 'the work will always be there' when weighed against life she actually wants.
rationales.stimulation: The trip is full of novelty and vivid experience: Goa, a 'tiny beach shack,' sunset shots, and sketching 'the way light hits water.'
rationales.hedonism: She emphasizes sensory pleasure and enjoyment with 'best fish curry I've ever had,' 'Zero regrets,' and 'doing things that feel good.'
rationales.conformity: She explicitly rejects the standard script of 'accumulate stuff' and treats that as less meaningful than the life she wants.
rationales.benevolence: She frames the trip around 'people I love' and values time with 'the people who matter,' centering close relationships.
